perturbation.original_text: 1.60 "FTE" means (a) with respect to any full-time employee of BLI, [***], who is [***] dedicated to the development of Workflows under this Agreement, a total of twelve (12) months of [***] work conducted by such employee or (b) with respect to any full-time employee of BLI that is not [***] dedicated to the development of Workflows under this Agreement, a full time equivalent effort consisting of a minimum of a total of [***] ([***]) hours per year of work [***] a Workflow Development Plan by such employee of a Party. In no event shall the work of one (1) employee exceed 1 full FTE.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.60 "FTE" means (a) with respect to any full-time employee of BLI, [***], who is [***] dedicated to the development of Workflows under this Agreement, a total of twelve (12) months of [***] work conducted by such employee or (b) with respect to any full-time *contractor* of BLI that is not [***] dedicated to the development of Workflows under this Agreement, a full time equivalent effort consisting of a minimum of a total of [***] ([***]) hours per year of work [***] a Workflow Development Plan by such employee of a Party. In no event shall the work of one (1) *contractor* exceed 1 full FTE.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'employee' is now being used interchangeably with 'contractor' within the same definition. While FTE is defined in terms of work contribution for full-time employees, this change introduces uncertainty as it might not align with the standard understanding of how a contractor's time is accounted for. This may cause contradiction in Section 5.4.1(c), where it is stated "BLI acknowledges and agrees that BLI FTEs (including Key Persons) are not employees or agents of Ginkgo".
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.4.1(c) Conduct on Site. BLI acknowledges and agrees that BLI FTEs (including Key Persons) are not employees or agents of Ginkgo, that Ginkgo has no responsibility to provide worker's compensation or other liability coverage, insurance, benefits or, other than as expressly provided in this Agreement, compensation for BLI FTEs. BLI further acknowledges and agrees that BLI FTEs are acting solely as representatives of BLI during any work performed at Ginkgo's facilities. BLI shall (a) comply, and shall cause each BLI FTE to comply, with all Applicable Laws; (b) abide, and shall cause each on-site BLI FTE to abide, by [***] guidelines and procedures related to Ginkgo's facilities and use of its foundries that are [***] (e.g., [***]) made available to BLI or BLI's FTEs; and (c) cause on-site BLI FTEs to execute a confidentiality agreement with Ginkgo in the form attached hereto as Exhibit C. Each BLI FTE will be required to complete training offered by Ginkgo regarding the guidelines and procedures referred to in Section 5.4.1(b) (Dedicated FTEs; Key Persons). Without limitation of the foregoing, each BLI FTE shall be responsible for performing work in such a manner as to [***].
perturbation.justification: The modification introduces 'contractor' in the definition of FTE, which contradicts Section 5.4.1(c) stating 'BLI FTEs (including Key Persons) are not employees or agents of Ginkgo'. By including contractors in the FTE definition, it creates ambiguity and potential conflict with the understanding that FTEs are not employees. Since the original agreement specifically defined BLI FTEs as not employees or agents, explicitly introducing contractors in their definition creates a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4. APPLICABILITY OF BLI TERMS AND CONDITIONS. With respect to Beacon Optofluidic Machines (including related Hardware and Software), Consumables and Services for which Ginkgo has placed a Purchase Order pursuant to this Agreement, the Parties agree to the BLI Terms and Conditions that apply with respect to Beacon Optofluidic Machines (including related Hardware and Software), Consumables and Services, unless, notwithstanding anything to the contrary set forth in the BLI Terms and Conditions (including any language regarding the treatment of additional or different terms set forth therein), a term in such BLI Terms and Conditions is inconsistent with a term in this Agreement, in which case this Agreement shall control, including as follows:
perturbation.changed_text: 4. APPLICABILITY OF BLI TERMS AND CONDITIONS. With respect to Beacon Optofluidic Machines (including related Hardware and Software), Consumables and Services for which Ginkgo has placed a Purchase Order pursuant to this Agreement, the Parties agree to the BLI Terms and Conditions that apply with respect to Beacon Optofluidic Machines (including related Hardware and Software), Consumables and Services, unless, notwithstanding anything to the contrary set forth in the BLI Terms and Conditions (including any language regarding the treatment of additional or different terms set forth therein), a term in such BLI Terms and Conditions is inconsistent with a term in this *Exhibit*, in which case this *Exhibit* shall control, including as follows:
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Agreement' to 'Exhibit' creates confusion. The main body of the agreement is meant to supersede contradictory terms in the BLI Terms and Conditions. By replacing 'Agreement' with 'Exhibit,' it incorrectly suggests that only a specific exhibit within the agreement overrides the BLI terms, potentially leading to disputes over which terms take precedence.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1.5 any disputes between the Parties related to Beacon Optofluidic Machines (including related Hardware and Software), Consumables, Services or the activities under this Agreement (including the performance of the Workflow Development Plans) shall be governed by Section 14.5 (Governing Law; Dispute Resolution; Equitable Remedies) of this Agreement, including disputes as to whether (a) [***], (b) [***] or (c) (i) [***], (ii) [***] or (iii) [***], and, with respect to each of (a), (b) and (c), which Ginkgo shall be entitled to dispute in writing in good faith irrespective of whether BLI is given sole or final decision-making authority with respect thereto under the applicable BLI Terms and Conditions;
perturbation.justification: The original text prioritizes the entire agreement over the BLI terms and conditions. By changing 'Agreement' to 'Exhibit', the modified text creates a contradiction. The 'Contradicted Text' specifies that disputes related to the agreement (including Beacon Optofluidic Machines, Consumables, Services, etc.) are governed by Section 14.5 of *this Agreement*. The modified text, however, implies that only a specific exhibit, not the entire agreement, overrides BLI terms, creating ambiguity as to which terms take precedence in the event of a dispute. This introduces an in-text contradiction and potentially undermines the clause specifying dispute resolution for the entire agreement.

perturbation.original_text: 9.1.1 Scope of Grants. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, and in consideration for the payments to BLI under this Agreement, during the Term, BLI, on behalf of itself and its Affiliates, hereby grants and shall grant to Ginkgo a non-exclusive, sublicensable (solely in accordance with Section 9.1.4 (Consent to Sublicense)), non-transferable, non-royalty-bearing (subject to Section 13.3.2 (Effects of Termination Based Upon Ginkgo Buy-Down Election)) worldwide license in, to and under (i) BLI Background IP, and (ii) other Intellectual Property that is Controlled by BLI and that is [***] BLI Inventions, with both of (i) and (ii) being limited to what is necessary for Ginkgo to [***] and (iii) the Collaboration Intellectual Property solely to: (a) perform research [***] on biological entities, including organisms, cells and strains (and sub-components thereof); (b) (i) design and develop (A) Collaboration Workflows as generally contemplated under a Workflow Development Plan and (B) Ginkgo Workflows as permitted under this Agreement and (ii) use [***] Workflows to conduct the activities set forth in clause (a) and clause (c) of this Section 9.1.1 (Scope of Grants); (c) perform commercial research [***] and other Commercial Services for Third Parties; and (d) in each case of clauses (a) through (c) of this Section 9.1.1 (Scope of Grants), the license granted is for activities solely within the Licensed Field.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.1.1 Scope of Grants. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, and in consideration for the payments to BLI under this Agreement, during the Term, BLI, on behalf of itself and its Affiliates, hereby grants and shall grant to Ginkgo a non-exclusive, sublicensable (solely in accordance with Section 9.1.4 (Consent to Sublicense)), non-transferable, non-royalty-bearing (subject to Section 13.3.2 (Effects of Termination Based Upon Ginkgo Buy-Down Election)) worldwide license in, to and under (i) BLI Background IP, and (ii) other Intellectual Property that is Controlled by BLI and that is [***] BLI Inventions, with both of (i) and (ii) being limited to what is necessary for Ginkgo to [***] and (iii) the Collaboration Intellectual Property solely to: (a) perform research [***] on biological entities, including organisms, cells and strains (and sub-components thereof); (b) (i) design and develop (A) Collaboration Workflows as generally contemplated under a Workflow Development Plan and (B) Ginkgo Workflows as permitted under this Agreement and (ii) use [***] Workflows to conduct the activities set forth in clause (a) and clause (c) of this Section 9.1.1 (Scope of Grants); (c) perform commercial research [***] and other Commercial Services for *any* Third Parties; and (d) in each case of clauses (a) through (c) of this Section 9.1.1 (Scope of Grants), the license granted is for activities solely within the *Unlicensed* Field.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Third Parties' to 'any Third Parties', implying even those within the 'Excluded Fields' and simultaneously changing 'Licensed Field' to 'Unlicensed Field' in the final clause, completely contradicts the stated purpose of this section. The purpose of this license is to give Ginkgo the possibility of performing commercial research and other commercial services within the 'Licensed Field' which excludes the 'Excluded Fields'
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.93 "Licensed Field" means any and all organisms, products, fields and uses other than the Excluded Fields.
perturbation.justification: The original text grants Ginkgo a license to perform commercial research within the 'Licensed Field', which is defined as everything except the 'Excluded Fields'. The changed text modifies this by changing 'Third Parties' to 'any Third Parties', which implies even those within the 'Excluded Fields' and simultaneously changing 'Licensed Field' to 'Unlicensed Field' in the final clause. This creates a direct contradiction because it implies that Ginkgo is now licensed to operate in the 'Unlicensed Field' which includes the 'Excluded Fields', directly contradicting the purpose of the license which was to grant Ginkgo rights only outside of the 'Excluded Fields'. Therefore, the change introduces a strong in-text contradiction.
